title: Imported targets versus alias targets
description: An imported target represents a pre-built external dependency, while an alias gives an existing target a stable namespaced name for consumers.
body: Use when reading why a dependency is referenced as Foo::foo. Do not assume a namespaced name always means an external package. find_package and FetchContent expose imported targets carrying a dependency's INTERFACE properties; add_library(... ALIAS ...) lets a project export its own targets under a namespace so internal and packaged consumers use one name. Inspect the defining CMake to tell which it is. Validation signals include consumers linking the same Foo::foo name whether built in-tree or found externally. Risk boundary: linking the raw target name instead of the alias couples consumers to layout, so prefer the namespaced name.
